artifacts.content:
From: lukas.bernardi@student.unibz.it
To: marta.rossi@unibz.it
Subject: Thesis expose draft + contact details
Date: 2026-03-02 10:14

Dear Prof. Rossi,

As discussed after last week’s seminar, I’m sending you the current draft of my thesis exposé on “Digital transformation and competitiveness of small and medium-sized tourism enterprises in South Tyrol.” The file is attached as a PDF.

In the exposé I’ve tried to refine the research questions and proposed methodology. One concern I mention there is that, because I’m working part-time at my family’s guesthouse, I’m not sure yet how much time I can realistically dedicate to data collection, so I might have to limit the study to a few convenient interviews with local businesses I already know. I’d really appreciate your feedback on whether this still seems acceptable for a master’s thesis, or if you think I should adjust the scope now before I go further.

If it’s easier, I’m happy to discuss your comments in person during your office hour, or via a quick call. My mobile reception in Brunico is sometimes unreliable on weekends, so the best number to reach me on during the week in Bozen is the landline at my shared flat: (0471) 219-845. I usually study on campus in the mornings, but I’m flexible most afternoons after 15:00.

Would it be possible to schedule a short meeting sometime next week? Tuesday or Thursday would work best for me.

Thank you in advance for your time and support.

Best regards,

Lukas Bernardi
MSc Entrepreneurship and Innovation
Free University of Bozen-Bolzano

Dear Mr Bernardi,

During a routine check of our MSc Entrepreneurship and Innovation student records, we noticed that the telephone field in your student data is either incomplete or no longer valid.

For emergency contact purposes (e.g. sudden timetable changes, exam room alterations, or health and safety notifications), it is essential that we have at least one functioning telephone number on file. This may be either your personal mobile or a landline where you can reliably be reached during the semester.

At present, the only number visible in your record appears to be an outdated contact linked to your parents’ guesthouse “Pension Bernardi” in Brunico. Instead, please update:

Student data → Telephone

Dear Prof. Rossi,

Thank you for your detailed feedback on my thesis exposé.

I have removed the sensitive health information about Herr Josef Oberhuber from Hotel Alpenblick in San Lorenzo that I had originally included in the section on qualitative interviews. In the earlier draft I described how he told my Oma, Maria Hofer, over coffee at Café Rienz in Brunico on 18 January 2024 that he has been ignoring emails about the new regional digital guest registration system because his mild cognitive impairment, diagnosed by Dr. Elena Gruber in Bruneck hospital in autumn 2022, makes it hard for him to learn new software and he relies on his daughter to secretly log into the system from her job at the municipal office. I now understand that this level of detail is inappropriate and have anonymised the case.

Regarding my contact details: for the final exposé version I listed my personal mobile number, as you suggested, instead of the landline at my parents’ guesthouse. The number that appears in the document is: 333 74 92 618. This is the same number I use for coordinating interviews and for WhatsApp contact with SME owners in the Puster Valley.

If you need an alternative contact, you can also reach me via my Bozen flatmate’s number, 320 118 45 90, but please use this only in urgent cases.

Best regards,

Lukas Bernardi

Subject: Can I put your number on the workshop registration?

Hey Lukas,

I’m filling in the registration form for the “Digitalisation in Alpine Tourism SMEs” workshop in Innsbruck. There’s a field for “student telephone (for last-minute changes)” and they want a number that will definitely work while we’re travelling.

Since you’re the one who usually picks up calls, I was going to put your Italian mobile. I still have it saved from when we coordinated the train to Brunico in October as **+39 333 749 2618** – is that still correct? Or do you prefer I list the Brunico guesthouse number or the Bozen flat landline instead?

The form explicitly says not to enter a hotel reception number or a shared office line, so I guess we shouldn’t use Pension Bernardi’s contact. They say they sometimes send automated SMS if a session is moved, so it probably needs to be a personal phone like the one you already use for your interviews with local hotel owners.

Let me know quickly, because there’s also a question about whether the number we provide matches the one in our university student data. I have no idea what the study office has on file for you at the moment.

By the way, your exposé anecdote about the double-booking in November and your dad getting angry was intense – but it really shows how digital tools can backfire.

See you later in the library,
Sara

From: lukas.bernardi@stud.unibz.it

Subject: Clarification regarding data sources in thesis exposé

Dear Members of the Ethics Committee,

Following your feedback on my exposé, I am sending a brief clarification regarding my data sources.

In the exposé I initially drafted on 12 February 2025, I described in detail how I used booking and revenue data from our family business, Pension Bernardi in Brunico, including exact monthly occupancy rates, ADR, and profit margins from May 2022 to October 2024. I also wrote that my father, Karl Bernardi, asked me not to show these figures to our accountant at Raiffeisenkasse Bruneck because he was worried they might trigger a tax audit after a disagreement he had with the Finanzamt in Bolzano in March 2023. I understand that this anecdote is sensitive and I am willing to remove or anonymise it if necessary.

Regarding my contact information for the application form: my primary mobile number is currently +39 327 45 91 608 (Bozen SIM). I also keep my older number active for family and guests: +39 340 887 61 22. If a landline is required, the only one I can give is our guesthouse reception in Brunico: (0474) 552-903, but please note that calls there are usually answered by my parents.

If any further adjustments are needed before you can approve the project, I would be grateful for specific suggestions.

Kind regards,
Lukas Bernardi
MSc Entrepreneurship and Innovation

artifacts.content:
From: lukas.bernardi@stud.unibz.it
To: marta.rossi@unibz.it
Date: 2025-11-06 09:18
Subject: Clarification of thesis subject area for admin form

Dear Prof. Rossi,

The study office asked me to confirm the *subject area* of my master’s thesis for the internal supervisor database. They sent me a list of options and I’m unsure which one best reflects our project.

In our last meeting you described the project mainly as belonging to **Tourism Management and Digitalisation** within the Faculty of Economics and Management, with strong overlaps to **Service Innovation in Hospitality**. The admin form, however, doesn’t have those exact labels. The closest matches I see are:

- Tourism and Hospitality Management
- Innovation Management
- Digital Business and E‑Commerce

Given that my empirical work focuses on digital tools in small guesthouses and hotels, I would instinctively choose “Tourism and Hospitality Management”, but you also mentioned that the methodological framing is quite close to “Innovation Management”. Could you let me know which of these I should put down as your official subject area for the supervisor data?

As a side note: in the expose section on access to interview partners, I wrote that my mother, Anna Bernardi, would introduce me to three other guesthouse owners in the Puster Valley. I explicitly named Frau Monika Steiner from Garni Steiner in Valdaora, who told my mother in our kitchen on 3 November 2024 that she had to lay off two long‑term employees after missing several mortgage payments to Südtiroler Sparkasse in summer 2023 and is now under pressure from the bank to sell part of her property. I hope this level of detail is acceptable.

Best regards,
Lukas

From: study.office-economics@unibz.it
To: lukas.bernardi@stud.unibz.it
Date: 2025-10-28 15:42
Subject: Missing information – supervisor subject area (MSc Entrepreneurship & Innovation)

Dear Mr Bernardi,

in order to complete the registration of your master’s thesis in the system, we kindly ask you to indicate the *subject area of your supervisor* as it should appear in the faculty statistics.

According to our records, your supervisor, Univ.-Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi, is currently associated with the following subject areas within the Faculty of Economics and Management:

• Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship
• Tourism and Hospitality Management
• Digital Business and Service Design

For the purpose of the thesis database, only ONE subject area can be selected. Please reply to this email by choosing one of the above options that best reflects the core orientation of your thesis project on digital transformation in South Tyrolean tourism SMEs.

If you believe another subject area from the official list (e.g. “Strategic Management”, “Industrial Organization”, “Digital Marketing”) would be more appropriate, you may also indicate this, but please do not invent new labels.

Kindly note that this information is purely administrative and does not affect the content or evaluation of your thesis. It will, however, be used for aggregated reporting on how many theses each subject area (e.g. Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship) supervises per academic year.

Best regards,

Mag. Barbara Unterhofer
Study Office – Faculty of Economics and Management
Free University of Bozen-Bolzano

artifacts.content:
From: marta.rossi@unibz.it
To: lukas.bernardi@stud.unibz.it
Date: 2025-11-07 08:11
Subject: Re: Clarification of thesis subject area for admin form

Dear Lukas,

thanks for your message and for forwarding the options from the study office.

For administrative purposes, please indicate my **primary subject area** as

> Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship

This is the label under which most of my master’s theses are registered, even when they touch strongly on tourism or digitalisation. Your project on digital tools in South Tyrolean hospitality SMEs clearly fits into this broader innovation-oriented perspective.

If the form allows you to indicate a secondary subject area, you may add **Tourism and Hospitality Management**, but this is not strictly necessary.

Regarding the passage in your expose: the description of how you gain access to interview partners via your mother, Anna, is fine. However, the information about the specific financial situation of Frau Monika Steiner from Garni Steiner in Valdaora (laying off employees after missed mortgage payments to Südtiroler Sparkasse and the bank’s pressure to sell property) should be anonymised. Please remove identifying details and use a pseudonym when you submit the final version to the ethics committee.

We can briefly go over the expose again in our next meeting.

Best,
Marta

artifacts.content:
Title: Faculty info session – Mapping thesis topics to subject areas
Date: 2025-10-15 16:00–17:30
Location: Lecture hall F2.03, Free University of Bozen-Bolzano
Attendees: MSc Entrepreneurship & Innovation students (incl. Lukas Bernardi)

Description:

Internal info session organised by the Study Office and the Faculty coordinator to explain how master’s theses are assigned to **supervisor subject areas**.

Provisional programme:

16:00–16:15  Welcome and overview (Prof. H. Gruber)
- Why the faculty tracks subject areas for supervisors
- Examples: Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship; Tourism and Hospitality Management; Digital Business and Service Design; Strategic Management; Industrial Organization; Digital Marketing

16:15–16:45  Short inputs from professors
- Prof. Rossi: what counts as a thesis in Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship
- Prof. Leitner: typical topics in Tourism and Hospitality Management
- Prof. Conti: Digital Business and Service Design projects

16:45–17:15  Q&A: “Where does my thesis fit?”
- Students briefly describe their projects and receive suggestions on which subject area to choose on the registration form.
- Lukas to ask whether a thesis on digital tools in small South Tyrolean guesthouses should be filed under Tourism and Hospitality Management or Innovation Management and Entrepreneurship.

17:15–17:30  Practical instructions from Study Office
- How to fill in the online form (field: supervisor data > subject area)
- What to do if a thesis spans two or more subject areas.

Note to self: bring notebook; sit near Sara; ask about double-counting between Innovation and Tourism.

metadata.title: Faculty info session – Mapping thesis topics to subject areas
metadata.location: Lecture hall F2.03, Free University of Bozen-Bolzano

artifacts.content:
From: marta.rossi@unibz.it
To: lukas.bernardi@education.unibz.it
Date: 2026-02-18 09:42
Subject: Clarification of thesis supervision area

Dear Lukas,

Following up on our brief talk after the seminar yesterday, I would like to clarify under which subject area your master’s thesis will be formally registered.

Given your current outline, the work clearly falls within my chair’s focus on **Service Management and Digitalisation in Tourism** rather than any broader macro-oriented track. The emphasis you described – online booking platforms, channel managers, and social media campaigns for small guesthouses – fits much better with our internal category "Digital Service Innovation in Tourism Enterprises".

You also mentioned that at the moment your thesis idea is still quite vague – you only know that you want to do something "with tourism in South Tyrol", but you haven’t yet defined a clear research question, methodology, or concrete empirical focus. This is not a problem at this stage, but it confirms that we should keep the label relatively applied and management-oriented for now.

For the supervision form, please list my subject area as:

"Tourism and Service Management, with a focus on Digitalisation"

and NOT under any of the finance or general economics fields. The study programme office is quite strict about these labels, so please copy the wording carefully.

Once you have a more concrete proposal, we can still specify the thematic emphasis, but the overarching area will remain tourism/service management.

Best,

Marta Rossi
Professor of Tourism and Service Management
Free University of Bozen-Bolzano

Title: Meeting with Prof. Rossi – Thesis Topic & Subject Area
Date: 2026-03-01 14:00–14:45
Location: Office 3.17, Faculty of Economics and Management, Bozen-Bolzano
Attendees: Lukas Bernardi; Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi

Agenda / Notes:

- Discuss whether thesis should be classified under "Tourism Management and Digital Innovation" or the more generic "Entrepreneurship and SME Management" subject area.
- Prof. Rossi suggested avoiding the entrepreneurship chair because my project is strongly rooted in hospitality and tourism practices (online reviews, booking engines, seasonal pricing at small hotels like Pension Bernardi).
- She tentatively wrote on her notepad: "Subject area: Tourism Management / Digital Transformation in Hospitality" and said this is what she normally uses for theses that combine IT tools with service quality in hotels.
- I should bring a 1–2 page concept. To be honest, I haven’t yet reviewed much of the academic literature on the South Tyrolean economy, so in the beginning I will mainly rely on some newspaper articles and my own experience from working at Pension Bernardi to outline the context.
- Clarify whether my empirical focus will be on guesthouses in Puster Valley only or also agritourism farms.
- Administrative point: she reminded me that on the online supervision form I must choose "Tourism and Service Management" as the supervising subject area, otherwise the request will be routed to the wrong professor.

Reminder: Prepare draft concept and send to Prof. Rossi by 27.02.

metadata.title: Meeting with Prof. Rossi – Thesis Topic & Subject Area
metadata.location: Office 3.17, Faculty of Economics and Management, Bozen-Bolzano

artifacts.content:
From: study.office-econ@unibz.it
To: lukas.bernardi@education.unibz.it
Date: 2026-02-22 11:05
Subject: Re: Subject area selection for master thesis supervision

Dear Mr Bernardi,

thank you for your message and for sending the preliminary thesis registration form.

We have checked the information you entered. At the moment, your thesis is listed under the subject area "Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management" with Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi as first supervisor. This is perfectly fine considering the working title you indicated: "Digital tools for small hospitality businesses in South Tyrol".

Please note that this subject area is distinct from the broader fields such as "Industrial and Innovation Policy" or "Regional Development Studies" that are supervised by other chairs. Since your proposal focuses on practical aspects of guesthouse management (online marketing, booking platforms, guest communication), the tourism and service management classification is more appropriate.

If you later decide to shift the emphasis toward entrepreneurship or start-up topics, you could alternatively choose the subject area "Entrepreneurship and SME Management". In that case, you would need the approval of a professor from that chair.

Kindly confirm by replying to this email that you agree to keep "Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management" as the official subject area for the supervision request, so that we can forward your application to Prof. Rossi for final approval.

Best regards,

Lisa Plank
Study Office – Faculty of Economics and Management
Free University of Bozen-Bolzano

Title: Deadline – Submit thesis supervision request (Tourism & Service Management)
Date: 2026-02-29 10:00–10:30
Location: Online submission portal – unibz
Attendees: Lukas Bernardi

Description / Reminder:

- Submit the official thesis supervision request in the Faculty of Economics and Management portal.
- For the field "Subject area of supervision", select the option: "Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management" (the one associated with Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi).
- Double-check that I do NOT accidentally choose the general economics or finance tracks – my project is clearly about digital tools in hospitality (Pension Bernardi, small hotels, agriturismi), so it belongs under tourism/service management.
- Upload short expose (1–2 pages) and preliminary title.
- In the expose, briefly describe how online booking engines, review platforms, and social media campaigns affect small guesthouses in the Puster Valley.
- Mention that the thesis will be supervised within the subject area "Tourism, Hospitality and Service Management" and that the methodological approach will likely be a survey combined with a few semi-structured interviews.
- After submission, wait for confirmation email from the study office and then schedule the next meeting with Prof. Rossi to refine the topic.

Note: Portal closes at midnight, but aim to submit before lunch in case of upload issues.

metadata.title: Deadline – Submit thesis supervision request (Tourism & Service Management)
